text.title: Go Standard Layout Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Go Standard Layout application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Handler functions decode the request (JSON, query params, headers) into primitives, call the service with those primitives, and encode the response. No business logic.
rule.rationale: Logic in handlers means it can only be tested via HTTP; pulling it down to service makes unit tests trivial.

rule.statement: Service functions hold all decisions: validation rules, branching, orchestration of multiple repos, side effects (calling external APIs). They take and return domain types — never HTTP or DB types.
rule.rationale: Business logic anywhere else duplicates; service is the testable core.

rule.statement: Repository functions own SQL queries (or ORM calls). They take primitives or domain structs and return domain structs. No HTTP types ever enter the repo.
rule.rationale: SQL leaking out of repos couples upper layers to schema details; changing the schema becomes a multi-file change.

rule.statement: Service packages do not import net/http, encoding/json, or chi/gin/echo. The compiler enforces this via golangci-lint depguard.
rule.rationale: Imports are the easiest leak vector; the lint enforcement is cheap.

rule.statement: Where service depends on repo, the interface is declared at the service (consumer) side, not at the repo (implementer) side. The repo package exports concrete types; service defines what subset it needs.
rule.rationale: Consumer-side interfaces let each service define only the methods it uses; producer-side interfaces bloat and couple.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
